subject: History
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the meaning of the Revolt of 1857
Learn about the causes of the revolt
Identify important leaders of the revolt
Understand how the revolt spread to different places
Know how the British suppressed the revolt
Understand the changes introduced after the revolt

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Revolt of 1857
topics_covered: Importance of the event in Indian history
student_activities: List reasons why people protest against rulers

lesson_plan.title: Causes of the Revolt
topics_covered: Other reasons for anger against British rule
student_activities: Write two causes of the revolt
Discuss why the cartridge issue angered soldiers

lesson_plan.title: Leaders of the Revolt
topics_covered: Nana Sahib and Begum Hazrat Mahal
student_activities: List important leaders of the revolt
Match leaders with the places they fought

lesson_plan.title: Spread of the Revolt
topics_covered: Beginning of the revolt in Meerut
Spread to Delhi, Kanpur, Lucknow and Jhansi
student_activities: Mark important centres of the revolt on a map
Discuss why the revolt spread quickly

lesson_plan.title: Suppression of the Revolt
student_activities: Write how the British controlled the revolt
Discuss the difficulties faced by the rebels

lesson_plan.title: Changes After the Revolt and Revision
topics_covered: End of East India Company rule
Beginning of direct rule by the British Crown
student_activities: Write one change introduced after the revolt
Participate in a short revision quiz

teacher_tip: Use storytelling about important leaders like Rani Lakshmibai to make the lesson more engaging and memorable for students.
